content: All new employees join TechNova on a probationary period of six months. During this period, the employee's performance, conduct, and cultural fit are assessed by the reporting manager. At the end of the probation period, the manager submits a confirmation recommendation to the HR department. Employees on probation are not eligible for certain benefits such as Work From Home and leave encashment.

TechNova has four types of employment. Permanent full-time employees work standard hours and receive full benefits with a notice period of 60 days. Probationary employees are in their first six months and have reduced benefits with a notice period of 30 days. Part-time employees work up to 20 hours per week, receive pro-rated benefits, and have a notice period of 30 days. Fixed-term contract employees are engaged for a defined project-based period and their notice period is as specified in the contract.

All offer letters are contingent on successful completion of background verification. TechNova conducts checks on educational qualifications, previous employment history, address, and criminal records. Any discrepancy found post-joining may lead to immediate termination of employment.

---

The standard workweek at TechNova is Monday through Friday, from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM IST. This totals 45 hours per week inclusive of a one-hour lunch break. All employees are expected to be available during core hours of 10:30 AM to 5:00 PM for meetings and collaboration.

Employees may opt for flexible start times between 8:00 AM and 10:30 AM with prior manager approval. Flexi-time is a privilege and not a right. It can be withdrawn if it negatively affects team deliverables or client commitments.

Attendance at office locations is recorded via biometric systems. Remote employees must mark attendance on the HRMS portal by 10:00 AM daily. Three or more unexplained late arrivals in a month will be flagged to the reporting manager. Habitual absenteeism, defined as more than three unplanned absences per quarter, will be treated as a performance concern and may be escalated to HR.

TechNova observes ten mandatory public holidays in 2024. These are Republic Day on January 26, Holi on March 25, Dr. Ambedkar Jayanti compensatory off on April 15, Ram Navami on April 17, Independence Day on August 15, Gandhi Jayanti on October 2, Dussehra compensatory off on October 14, Kannada Rajyotsava on November 1 for the Bengaluru office only, Diwali on November 15, and Christmas on December 25.

---

Employees are entitled to 12 days of Casual Leave per calendar year. Casual Leave is credited at the rate of one day per month on the first of each month. It is intended for short and unplanned personal needs. A maximum of three consecutive Casual Leave days are allowed at a time. Beyond three days, Earned Leave must be used. Casual Leave cannot be clubbed with Earned Leave or public holidays without manager approval. Unused Casual Leave lapses at the end of the year and cannot be carried forward or encashed. Employees on probation are entitled to Casual Leave on a pro-rata basis.

Employees are entitled to 12 days of Sick Leave per calendar year. Sick Leave is credited in full on January 1st of each year. It is meant to cover illness or medical emergencies. Any illness lasting more than two consecutive days requires a medical certificate from a registered doctor. Sick Leave cannot be availed immediately before or after a public holiday without medical certification. Unused Sick Leave lapses at the end of the year and cannot be carried forward or encashed. Employees who exhaust their Sick Leave balance may apply for Leave Without Pay or Earned Leave with manager approval.

Employees earn 1.5 Privilege Leave days for every completed month of service, resulting in 18 days per year for full-time employees. Earned Leave must be taken in a minimum block of three consecutive working days. Applications must be submitted at least seven working days in advance. The maximum carry-forward limit is 30 days and any excess days lapse unless encashed. Up to 15 Privilege Leave days can be encashed once per year during the April appraisal cycle. The encashment formula is Basic Salary divided by 26, multiplied by the number of days encashed.

TechNova complies fully with the Maternity Benefit Amendment Act of 2017. Female employees with at least 80 days of service in the last 12 months are eligible for maternity leave. The entitlement is 182 days, which is 26 weeks, for the first two live births. For the third child onwards the entitlement is 84 days which is 12 weeks. Employees expecting twins or adopting a child below the age of three months are also entitled to 84 days of maternity leave. Leave can commence up to eight weeks before the expected delivery date. TechNova also provides a Work From Home option for an additional one month after the maternity leave period ends. A creche facility or a monthly allowance of Rupees 3000 is provided for children below three years of age.

Male employees are entitled to five days of paid paternity leave. This leave must be availed within three months of childbirth or adoption. The application must be submitted in HRMS and a birth certificate or adoption certificate must be provided within 30 days.

In the event of the death of an immediate family member, employees are entitled to three consecutive working days of paid bereavement leave. Immediate family includes spouse, children, parents, and siblings. Situations involving extended family members are handled at the discretion of the reporting manager in consultation with HR.

In addition to the ten mandatory public holidays, employees may choose two Optional Holidays from a list published by HR at the start of each year. These are typically regional or religious festivals not covered in the mandatory holiday list. Optional Holidays must be applied for in advance and cannot be accumulated or carried forward to the next year.

When all leave balances are exhausted, employees may apply for Leave Without Pay with manager and HR approval. Leave Without Pay directly impacts the monthly salary and is deducted at the rate of Gross Salary divided by 26 per day. Excessive Leave Without Pay may be treated as a performance or conduct issue depending on the circumstances.

---

Employees who have completed their probation period and have a performance rating of Meets Expectations or above are eligible for Work From Home. New joiners in their first six months are required to be in the office full time. Employees on probation are not eligible for Work From Home.

Individual Contributors are allowed up to eight Work From Home days per month. They must be present in the office at least three days per week during Work From Home weeks. Team Leads and Managers are allowed up to four Work From Home days per month and must be present on the team's anchor days. Senior Managers and above have a flexible arrangement based on business needs and require approval from the Chief Human Resources Officer.

Employees must be available on video calls with camera on during core hours of 10:30 AM to 5:00 PM. The home office must have a reliable internet connection with a minimum speed of 25 Mbps. Work From Home is not a substitute for leave. If an employee is unwell on a Work From Home day, Sick Leave must be applied. Work From Home cannot be availed on days with scheduled client visits or mandatory office events. Employees working from a city other than their base location for more than five days require prior HR approval. TechNova provides a one-time Work From Home setup allowance of Rupees 10000 which is taxable for all confirmed employees.

---

Every TechNova employee is expected to uphold the highest standards of professional conduct. Integrity means acting honestly and transparently in all dealings. Respect means treating all colleagues, clients, and partners with dignity. Accountability means taking ownership of your work and decisions. Confidentiality means protecting company, client, and employee information at all times. Compliance means adhering to all applicable laws, regulations, and company policies.

The following behaviours are strictly prohibited at TechNova. Harassment, discrimination, or bullying of any kind is not tolerated. Disclosure of confidential or proprietary information to external parties is prohibited. Conflict of interest without prior written disclosure to HR is not allowed. Using company assets such as hardware, software, or internet for personal commercial purposes is prohibited. Misrepresentation of work hours, expenses, or deliverables is considered a serious offence. Possession or use of illegal substances on company premises will result in immediate disciplinary action.

Violations of the Code of Conduct are subject to disciplinary proceedings. Depending on the severity of the violation, actions may include a verbal warning, written warning, suspension without pay, demotion, or termination of employment. Serious violations such as fraud or harassment may result in immediate termination without prior warning.

---

TechNova follows a bi-annual performance review cycle. Mid-year check-ins are conducted in October and annual appraisals take place in April every year. All employees are assessed on a five-point rating scale.

A rating of five means Outstanding and comes with an increment in the range of 15 to 20 percent. A rating of four means Exceeds Expectations and comes with an increment of 10 to 15 percent. A rating of three means Meets Expectations and results in an increment of 6 to 10 percent. A rating of two means Needs Improvement and results in an increment of 0 to 5 percent. A rating of one means Unsatisfactory and results in no increment and the initiation of a Performance Improvement Plan.

Employees rated Unsatisfactory for two consecutive review cycles, or for one cycle with no improvement after a Performance Improvement Plan, may be separated from the organisation. The Performance Improvement Plan duration is typically 60 to 90 days and involves weekly check-ins with the manager and HR.

---

The compensation package at TechNova is structured as a Cost to Company amount broken into several components. Basic Salary forms 40 percent of the CTC and is the basis for Provident Fund, gratuity, and leave encashment calculations. House Rent Allowance forms 20 percent of CTC and is exempt under Section 10 of the Income Tax Act for employees living in rented accommodation. Special Allowance forms 20 percent of CTC and is fully taxable. The Flexible Benefit Plan forms 15 percent of CTC and covers reimbursements for medical, fuel, books, and telephone expenses. The Performance Bonus forms 5 percent of CTC and is paid annually based on the employee's performance rating and is variable.

All employees receive Group Health Insurance with a coverage of Rupees 5 lakhs per employee and family, covering spouse and up to two children. Group Term Life Insurance coverage is three times the annual CTC. Personal Accident Insurance coverage is two times the annual CTC.

TechNova contributes 12 percent of the Basic Salary towards Provident Fund, matching the employee's own 12 percent contribution. Gratuity is paid as per the Payment of Gratuity Act and is applicable after five years of continuous service.

Employees working from home receive an internet reimbursement of up to Rupees 1500 per month. Every confirmed employee receives a Learning and Development budget of Rupees 20000 per year which can be used for certifications and external courses. The Employee Referral Bonus is Rupees 25000 for successful referrals and is paid after the referred employee completes six months at TechNova.

---

Employees in Band 1, which includes Analyst and Associate roles, must serve a notice period of 30 days. Employees in Band 2, which includes Senior Associate and Lead roles, must serve 60 days. Employees in Band 3, which includes Manager roles, must serve 60 days. Employees in Band 4, which includes Senior Manager and above, must serve a notice period of 90 days.

Employees wishing to resign must submit a written resignation through HRMS and via email to the reporting manager and HR Business Partner. The notice period must be served in full unless a buyout is mutually agreed upon by the employee and the organisation. Notice period buyout is calculated at the rate of the last drawn gross salary divided by 30 per day.

Employees are required to complete knowledge transfer sessions with their replacement or team before their last working day. All company assets including laptop, access cards, and SIM cards must be returned on the last working day. An exit interview is mandatory and is conducted by HR. The final settlement including pending salary, leave encashment, and bonus is processed within 45 days of the last working day. The experience letter and relieving letter are issued within seven working days of full clearance.

TechNova may terminate employment for reasons including sustained poor performance, misconduct, redundancy, or business restructuring. In cases of termination without cause, employees are entitled to salary in lieu of the notice period. Termination for gross misconduct such as fraud, harassment, or theft may be immediate and without payment in lieu of notice.

---

TechNova is committed to providing a fair and respectful work environment. Employees who experience workplace issues, harassment, discrimination, or policy violations are encouraged to raise their concerns through the formal grievance process. In the first step, the employee should raise the concern directly with the immediate manager for non-manager related issues. In the second step, the employee submits a written grievance to the HR Business Partner via HRMS or email. In the third step, HR conducts a formal inquiry within ten working days. In the fourth step, the decision is communicated to the employee in writing with recommended action. In the fifth step, if the employee is unsatisfied, the matter can be escalated to the Chief Human Resources Officer.

TechNova has a zero-tolerance policy towards sexual harassment at the workplace in line with the Prevention of Sexual Harassment Act of 2013. An Internal Complaints Committee is constituted at each office location. Complaints can be submitted directly to the Internal Complaints Committee or through the anonymous ethics hotline at posh@technova.example.com. All complaints are treated with strict confidentiality and the identity of the complainant is protected throughout the process.

---
